ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON JAN-18-2006 IN 206, PLAINTIFF ALBERT RICE'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 5/8/06 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR 4/28/06 @ 9:30AM IN DEPT. 304. PLAINTIFF REPRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT THIS CASE IS AT ISSUE. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. MR. RICE'S DEPOSITION IS TO BE COMPLETED BY 2/3/06. PATHOLOGY TO BE PROVIDED TO BERRY & BERRY BY 2/9/06. PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO STANDARD FRICTION INTERROGATORIES MUST BE SERVED WITHIN ONE WEEK OF THE DATE OF THIS HEARING. EXPERT DISCOVERY AND DEPOSITIONS TO BE COMPLETED BY 5/1/06. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT APPLIES. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 3/3/06 AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ORDER SIGNED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Goldstein of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SCOTT LABORATORIES, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. In moving for summary judgment, defendant contends that decedent was not exposed to any asbestos containing materials supplied by defendant. Although defendant attaches plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories, defendant relies only on plaintiff's testimony to shift the burden. This is insufficient under Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal. App. 4th 1433. Defendant's objections to the Puccioni declaration are overruled. Plaintiff presents evidence that creates a triable issue of fact on the issue of exposure. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-06-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Anne Acuna of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Shelley Tinkoff of Brydon Hugo & Parker (808.0300) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on factually devoid discovery responses. Plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue of fact. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's evidence are sustained. Orders granting the motion, and on defendants evidentiary objections were signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
